english: taxes Tar., cf ˳bab ('bab); skyon ˳bébs-pa (skyon 'bebs pa) to impute a crime to a person, to calumniate Glr.; *(s)kad ṗab-cè* ((s)kad phab ce) W. to translate; blo (blo), resp. tugs (thugs), e.g. yul-ṗyogs ˳di-ru ˳bébs-pa (yul phyogs 'di ru 'bebs pa) to direct one's thoughts to a certain place, to have a mind to settle there; gtán-la ˳bébs-pa (gtan la 'bebs pa) v. gtan (gtan); *nā ṅúl-la ṗáb-cȧ* (na dngul la phab ca) to turn the barley into money Kun.
Cs.: 'a sort of plastic mass used by smiths'.
the hair on the forehead of a horse Cs.

Sch.: 'temperate, saving, economical; ˳bél-po ˳dug ('bel po 'dug) a good deal has been saved (by economy), ample provision has been made; ˳bél-du ˳júg-pa ('bel du 'jug pa) to enjoin temperance, frugality' (?).
a dry measure, which seems to be very variable as to quantity, and little used; k̇al-bó (khal 'bo) Cs. bushel.

pf. ˳bos ('bos), bo (bo), ṗo (pho), fut. dbo (dbo) W. *bo-cè* (bo ce), *ṗo-cè* (pho ce), to pour out, k̇rag ˳bó-ba (khrag 'bo ba) to shed blood Ma.; ma bó-ba byuṅ-nas (ma bo ba byung nas) there being no spilling Glr.; bdúd-rtsi ṗó-bas (bdud rtsi pho bas) pouring out nectar Glr.; *ṗos toṅ* (phos tong) Ld. pour out! — 2. to swell (up), to rise, *bós-te rag* (bos te rag) I see it has swelled W.; ˳bós-pai nas ('bos pa'i nas) Sch. swelled barley; srán-ma ṗós-pa tsam (sran ma phos pa tsam) as big as a swelled pea Lt.; srád-ma ṗos-ṗós (srad ma phos phos) grain swelled, and afterwards parched. — 3. to sprout, shoot forth, of wild-growing plants, sa ˳bo ˳dug (sa 'bo 'dug) the ground is verdant C.

a kind of upper-garment, ṗo-˳bóg (pho 'bog), for men, mo-˳bóg (mo 'bog) for females Cs. — 2. W.: a square cloth, for wrapping up and carrying provisions, also *bog-čá* (bog cha), hence *bog-čes* (bog chas) a burden thus formed. — 3. W., a small hillock; *sa-bóg* (sa bog), *be-bóg* (be bog) a sand-hill; *ri-bóg* (ri bog) a projecting hill, also a clod; *paṅ-bóg* (pang bog) a piece of turf.
żwá-mo ˳bog-tó (zhwa mo 'bog to) Cs., hat with a broad crown of yellow cloth, and trimmed with long-haired fur.

pf. bog (bog), ṗog (phog), fut. dbog? (dbog) W. *boy-cè* (boy ce), to be rooted out, uprooted, pulled out, of teeth W.; to be put out of joint, tsigs (tshigs) W. — 2. to be taken down (opp. to ˳gél-ba ('gel ba)), k̇ál-rnams ṗog (khal rnams phog) Glr. the loads were taken off; *zaṅ mé-ne* (zangs me ne) the kettle from the fire W. — 3. to grow loose, to come off, to drop off, leaves from a tree C. — 4. to sink down, to fall to the ground, esp. in a fainting-fit, ˳bog-ciṅ brgyál-ba ('bog cing brgyal ba) Thgy., brgyál(-żiṅ) ˳bóg pa (brgyal zhing 'bog pa) Pth, id.; ˳bog yun-ríṅ-na ('bog yun ring na) Lt. prob.: when the fainting-fit has lasted a long time; smyo-˳bóg (smyo 'bog) madness, insanity, ˳byuṅ ('byung) sets in, takes place Glr.; ˳bog-śi ('bog shi) being quickly carried off, by cholera etc. W. — 5. to wade, to dip into, to submerge, ču-la (chu la) Dzl. also ču (chu) Lex. to wade through the water.

pf. ṗog (phog), fut. dbog (dbog), dbag (dbag), imp. ṗog (phog), 1. to give, to impart, gdams-ṅág (gdams ngag), luṅ (lung) counsel, advice, directions Tar.; k̇ríd, bsláb-pa (khrid, bslab pa) Mil. instruction; sdóm-pa (sdom pa) to impose religious duties, i.e. to receive into holy orders Glr.; to bequeath, to give(?), nor (nor) Lex. — 2. gżi-ma (gzhi ma) to fit up a dwelling, = ˳bébs-pa ('bebs pa) Glr.; gro ˳bógs-pa (gro 'bogs pa) to take breakfast. — 3. to blot, stain, pollute, v. ˳bág-pa ('bag pa).
Cs., roundness, rotundity, boṅ-˳bóṅ (bong 'bong), round; acc. to my informants *boṅ-bóṅ* (bong bong) loose, slack, incoherent W. —

bod-pa (bod pa), pf. imp. bos (bos), W. *bo-cè* (bo ce), bos (bos) (boi, bọ)* (boi, bo), 1. to call, to exclaim, sdod čig ces bós-so (sdod cig ces bos so) he exclaimed: wait! Dzl.; mi żig (mi zhig) B., mi żig-la (mi zhig la) col., to call a person; rtsar (rtsar) Glr., mdún-du (mdun du) Pth. to call near; ṅáṅ-du (nang du) to call in; ˳bód-pai brda ('bod pa'i brda) or tsig (tshig) interjection Gram; čáṅ-la ˳bod-pa (chang la 'bod pa) to call, to invite, to a cup of beer Dzl.; ma bós-par ˳óṅ-ba (ma bos par 'ong ba) to come uninvited Dzl ; lú-čos ˳bód-pa (lu chos 'bod pa) Wdṅ., ˳bod-grógs-pa ('bod grogs pa) Dzl. to cry repeatedly; *bós-ra* (bos ra) Ld., *boi-ra, bọ-ra* (boi ra, bo ra) Lh., *táṅ-cè* (tang ce) or gyáb-cè* (gyab ce) id. W.; ṅu-˳bód (ngu 'bod) howling, v. ṅú-ba (ngu ba). — 2. to call, to name, to denominate, yúl-skad... ˳bód-pa (yul skad ... 'bod pa) commonly called, styled ... Wdṅ.
